title: The code that works and means nothing
checkLabels.t6: The text is a real paragraph
checkLabels.t7: The bottom line is a paragraph
checkLabels.t8: No groups standing in for text
checkLabels.t10: No groups standing in for items
why.empty: The editor is empty. You deleted everything, the content included.
why.untouched: This is exactly the code I gave you. Nothing has been changed.
hint2.untouched: Do not delete anything. Swap the tags — same words, but in tags that say what each thing IS.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed.
hint2.not-closed:
If you swap a
for a

, swap the

for a

too. A leftover closing tag is the most common mistake here.
why.no-h1: There is still no real heading with text in it. <div class="big-title"> only looks like a heading. Google sees a group. The reader hears a group.
hint2.no-h1: Look at the line with the words Bikes for sale. That is the main heading of the page — and it is written as a group.
hint3.no-h1:
A similar case:
About us
becomes

About us

. The words stay, the tag changes.
hint4.no-h1: Swap the opening and closing tags around Bikes for sale. You no longer need the big-title class — drop it.
why.many-h1: There is more than one top-level heading. <h1> is the main heading of the page — and a page has only one main thing.
hint2.many-h1: The Bike Shop logo is not the heading of this page. It repeats on every page of the site. Leave it a group for now.
why.still-fake: There is still a group dressed up as something else — a div with the class big-title, text or menu-item. The class is a word you invented. The browser does not understand it.
hint2.still-fake: Search the editor with Ctrl+F: big-title, class="text", menu-item. Every hit is a tag that has to change.
hint3.still-fake:
Dropping the class is not enough.
Hello
without the class is still a group. The tag is wrong, not the label.
hint4.still-fake:
Change the tag itself: a div with class text becomes

, a div with class big-title becomes

, a div with class menu-item becomes
  • .
why.no-p: There is no real paragraph holding the 1998 sentence. <div class="text"> is a group that contains text — not text.
hint2.no-p: The sentence We have been selling bikes since 1998 is a paragraph. There is a tag for exactly that, and you have known it since lesson 17.
hint3.no-p:
A similar case:
Open until 6pm.
becomes

Open until 6pm.

.
hint4.no-p: Swap the div around the sentence for a p. Watch the closing tag too.
why.no-p-footer: The bottom line with the copyright is still not a paragraph. It is text as well, not a group.
why.no-list: The menu is still not a list. Home, Bikes and Contact are three things of one kind — that is a <ul> with three <li>. You have known that since lesson 26.
hint2.no-list: Three groups stacked up are not a list. The reader does not say list of 3 items, it says group, group, group.
hint3.no-list:
A similar case: three
Monday
become a
    with three
  • Monday
    • .
hint4.no-list: Wrap the three items in a <ul>. Each one becomes an <li>. The outer menu group can stay for now.
blocks.0.text: Look at the code on the right. Run it. It works. It comes out exactly as it should.
blocks.1.text: And it is one of the worst pieces of code you will see in this course.
blocks.3.text: Eleven <div>. Zero meaning. Everything on this page is a group — the heading is a group, the menu is a group, the paragraph is a group.
blocks.4.text: The code says: group, group, group, group. Nothing more.
blocks.5.text: If someone read this code aloud to you, you would not know what kind of page it is.
blocks.7.text: Google reads the page and looks for the heading. There is no heading — there is a group labelled big-title. It does not know what that label means. You invented it.
blocks.8.text: A blind person wants to skip the menu. There is no menu — there is a group. They want to jump to the next section by heading. There are no headings.
blocks.9.text: For them your page is one long, shapeless wall of text.
blocks.11.text: Six months from now you open this file. What is <div class="content">? Well... content. Which content? Of what?
blocks.12.text: The code tells you nothing, because you told it nothing.
blocks.13.text: It even has a name: div soup. Search for it and you will find thousands of people complaining — usually about their own code from a year ago.
blocks.15.text: The rule is one sentence: ask what a thing IS, not what it looks like.
blocks.16.text:
Is it a heading? Then it is

. A paragraph?

. A menu? A list. A list of things?

    .
blocks.17.text: There is a tag for almost everything. <div> is what you use WHEN THERE IS NOT.
blocks.18.text: Fix the code on the right. Do not touch the styling — there is none. Swap the tags for meaningful ones.
blocks.19.text: And look at what happens to the preview: almost nothing. It looks nearly the same.
blocks.20.text: That is exactly what makes it treacherous. The difference cannot be seen with eyes. It is seen by Google, by the screen reader, and by you in six months.
blocks.21.text: The three who do not look at the screen.
